commit.message:
fix: replace deprecated ReadAction.compute/run with ApplicationManager.runReadAction

Replace all usages of deprecated ReadAction.compute() and ReadAction.run()
with ApplicationManager.getApplication().runReadAction() across plugin-core
and plugin-experimental. These APIs are deprecated in IntelliJ 2026.1 (IU-261).

Also replace deprecated JBPopupFactory.createListPopupBuilder(JBList) with
createPopupChooserBuilder(List) in ChatToolWindowContent.kt, using the new
Consumer<T> callback instead of Runnable.

Document intentional use of experimental/scheduled-for-removal APIs in
PlatformApiCompat where no non-experimental alternatives exist:
- LafManager.getInstalledThemes() (@ApiStatus.Experimental, no alternative)
- DataPack via DataPackChangeListener (scheduled-for-removal, but using the
  internal DATA_PACK_REFRESH alternative would cause a build FAILURE)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: add explicit null byte rejection to PathJail.Validate

PathJail.Validate relied on Go 1.21+ syscall-layer null byte rejection,
which made the test non-deterministic. Add explicit strings.ContainsRune
check for null bytes before any filesystem operation (CWE-158).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: pre-create ~/.pluginVerifier/extracted-plugins before verifyPlugin

Plugin verifier 1.401 crashes with NoSuchFileException when this
directory doesn't exist (it tries to delete it unconditionally).
gradle/actions/setup-gradle v6 no longer caches ~/.pluginVerifier,
exposing the bug.

fix: correct reword undo type and strip CR from agent output

commit.message:
feat(agents): add AI agents with LangChain.js and HOL compliance

- PlayDetectionAgent: polls Spotify/Last.fm, resolves ISRCs via MusicBrainz,
  publishes verified play events to HCS PLAY_EVENTS_TOPIC
- DistributionAgent: subscribes via HCS-10, checks disputes, looks up on-chain
  splits, triggers RoyaltyVault.distribute(), confirms payments on HCS
- DisputeResolutionAgent: monitors DISPUTES_TOPIC, cross-references ISRC
  ownership via MusicBrainz, auto-resolves or escalates
- ArtistAdvisorAgent: primary user-facing agent with ConversationSummaryBuffer
  Memory; handles registration, earnings history, split management, dispute filing
- HOLRegistryClient: registers all four agents using @hashgraphonline/standards-sdk
  (HCS-11 profile) with deterministic fallback DIDs
- A2AMessageBus: HCS-10 inter-agent messaging for all four A2A flows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite JellyseerrService as comprehensive Jellyseerr/Overseerr API client

Bug fixes:
- Remove 'using' on HttpClient from IHttpClientFactory (prevents socket exhaustion)
- Fix status code 2 (pending) falling through to 'not_requested' via MapStatus()
- Add URL validation (http/https only) to prevent SSRF attacks
- Validate serviceType (radarr/sonarr) and mediaType (movie/tv) parameters

New features:
- 4K support via Is4k flag on RequestPayload
- Root folder selection via GetRootFoldersAsync()
- Language profiles via GetLanguageProfilesAsync() (Sonarr only)
- Tags via GetTagsAsync()
- Season-specific requests with int[] instead of object
- Server info via GetServiceServersAsync()
- Request quota via GetRequestCountAsync()
- Media lookup via GetMediaInfoAsync()
- Full model classes for all API responses

Safety improvements:
- SSRF prevention with URL scheme validation
- CancellationToken and ConfigureAwait(false) on all async paths
- Comprehensive try/catch with structured logging
- XML doc comments on all public members
- Input sanitization on all URL-interpolated parameters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cross-platform path traversal detection and WSL test resilience

Security: replace filepath.ToSlash with strings.ReplaceAll for backslash
normalization in containsDotDot() and IsSensitivePath(). filepath.ToSlash
is a no-op on Linux (backslash is not os.PathSeparator), allowing
Windows-style traversal paths like ..\etc\passwd to bypass detection.

WSL: add timeout and WSL_SKIP escape hatch to mage testwsl target.
cmdOutputTimeout helper prevents hangs when WSL is unresponsive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement real-time process lifecycle monitoring via eBPF (#53)

* feat(probes): implement real-time process lifecycle monitoring via eBPF

- Life-cycle tracking: Monitor the full lifespan of a process from creation
  (exec_watch) to destruction (do_group_exit).
- Precise longevity: Capture the exact duration of short-lived processes in
  milliseconds by synchronizing timestamps between exec and exit.
- Contextual linkage: Track cgroup_id to map process lifecycles to specific
  container workloads in Kubernetes/AI training environments.
- Death cause analysis: Enhance exit observability by distinguishing
  between MANUAL_SIGKILL and OOM_TERMINATED through kernel-level victim tagging.
- Reliability: Resolve 'bash' context races and kernel-version dependency
  by implementing name caching and Kprobe-based exit interception.

* lint

* allow dead code in vmlinux structs for CI

* feat: Add process lifecycle monitoring with eBPF

- Implement full-path lifecycle tracking (exec → exit)
- Add intelligent OOM vs manual kill discrimination
- Resolve race conditions with process name caching
- Capture precise process duration metrics
- Include cgroup_id for Kubernetes Pod correlation
- Update Helm chart to support process_lifecycle probe

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: Remove unused exit_signal field from ProcessExitEvent

- Remove exit_signal field to prevent uninitialized memory leaks
- Signal information is already available in exit_code (exit_code & 0x7f)
- Add padding field to maintain struct alignment

* exit_signal delete

* fix(process_lifecycle): fix OOM victim PID extraction and ppid marker

- fix: ctx.arg::<u32>(0) was reading oom_control* pointer as raw u32 (garbage value)
  now follows oom_control.chosen -> task_struct.tgid pointer chain via bpf_probe_read_kernel
- fix: OOM victim was setting ppid=0 instead of 0xDEAD, causing userspace to always
  classify OOM kills as MANUAL_SIGKILL
- add: oom_control struct definition to vmlinux.rs

* style: fix rustfmt alignment in oom_control struct

* style: fix rustfmt import order and line wrapping in process_lifecycle

* fix: use correct aya bpf_probe_read_kernel API signature

* fix: add process_lifecycle config and LLM metrics to telemetry

* Add OpenTelemetry metrics for LLM usage and performance (#56)

- Add `llm_requests_total` (Counter) to track success/error rates.
- Add `llm_tokens_total` (Counter) with prompt/completion labels.
- Add `llm_latency_seconds` (Histogram) to monitor request duration.

* fix: remove duplicate struct fields and function in telemetry

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Sam.An <56215891+sammiee5311@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: backfill username_hash for legacy users on startup

- Add backfill_username_hashes() to construct-db: fetches all rows
  WHERE username IS NOT NULL AND username_hash IS NULL, computes hash
  via caller-provided closure, sets username_hash and clears plaintext
- Call backfill at user-service startup after migrations (idempotent)
- After first deploy with USERNAME_HMAC_SECRET set, all legacy plaintext
  usernames are hashed and cleared automatically

commit.message:
feat: privacy-first HMAC username hashing

- Add migration 034: username_hash BYTEA column + unique index
- construct-crypto: hash_username() via HMAC-SHA256 (trim+lowercase normalization)
- construct-config: USERNAME_HMAC_SECRET env var in SecurityConfig
- construct-db: User struct gets username_hash field; all DB functions
  updated — create_user_passwordless, create_user_with_first_device,
  update_user_username now take Option<&[u8]> hash instead of plaintext;
  get_user_by_username_hash() added as primary lookup
- construct-user-service: compute hash server-side before all DB calls,
  check_username_availability uses hash lookup, plaintext never stored
- construct-auth-service: devices.rs uses hash for registration + collision check
- user-service binary: update_user_profile uses hash for collision check + update
- auth-service: recovery.rs lookup by UUID or username_hash with legacy plaintext fallback
- Fix auth_tests.rs and queue_test.rs missing username_hmac_secret field
- Update .sqlx offline query cache

Plaintext username column kept for now (migration 035 will drop it after
one-time backfill). At DB leak only HMAC hashes are exposed — irreversible
without server_secret.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use `copilot login` for Copilot CLI auth

`copilot auth login` is not a valid subcommand — the CLI treats
unrecognised subcommands as AI prompts, producing "Invalid command
format". The correct command is `copilot login`.

Also fixes `startCopilotLogin()` (terminal fallback) to use the
same resolved command instead of the hardcoded wrong string.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add raw timings, diffs and save metrics (#2333)

* feat: add raw timings, diffs and save metrics

Record and format build durations in Builder (src/Builder.php): compute step and total durations, output human-readable strings (ms or s) and store a duration_raw value in ms for comparisons. In Build command (src/Command/Build.php) load previous metrics from tmp/metrics.json, compute per-step diffs, annotate durations with colored +/- deltas when significant, render the metrics table with prepared rows, and persist the current metrics for the next run. This improves visibility into build timings and enables run-to-run comparisons.

* Update src/Command/Build.php

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor duration formatting to eliminate duplication in Builder (#2334)

refactor: use Util::convertDuration() to eliminate duplicated duration formatting

commit.message:
feat: scaffold Credit Spread Analysis & Prediction Platform

- config/settings.py: FRED series, Yahoo tickers, model params, paths
- src/data/fetcher.py: FRED + Yahoo ingestion with Parquet caching
- src/features/engineering.py: lags, rolling stats, momentum, z-scores, targets
- src/models/regime.py: HMM and GMM regime detection
- src/models/statistical.py: Granger causality, VAR, IRF, FEVD, Johansen
- src/models/ml_models.py: XGBoost/LightGBM/RF with TimeSeriesSplit + SHAP
- src/models/dl_models.py: LSTM and Transformer encoder with early stopping
- src/analysis/leading_indicator.py: spread-signal backtesting and metrics

perf: rewrite trimDots to avoid repeated string allocations (#8154)

* perf: rewrite trimDots to use index scanning instead of repeated string allocation

The old implementation created a new string on each iteration via substr/slice.
The new implementation scans for the first and last non-dot indices using
charCodeAt, then performs a single substring call. If no trimming is needed,
the original string is returned directly (zero allocations).

Benchmark (20M calls across 10 representative inputs):
- Old: ~628 ms
- New: ~154 ms
- ~4x faster (75% reduction)

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/pubkey/rxdb/sessions/291bb058-424b-40b5-a64d-5f29c3bdd2bd

commit.message:
feat(collab): crdt-safe Y.Map element storage

- Replace Y.Array('elements') with Y.Map('elementsMap') + Y.Array('elementOrder')
  for keyed, CRDT-safe element storage (no duplicate-on-concurrent-delete/insert)
- Shallow-clone elements on Y.Map write ({ ...el }) to snapshot versionNonce
  at write time; prevents Excalidraw's in-place mutations from making stored
  references always match the latest state (causing 0 changes detected)
- Shallow-clone elements on Y.Map read for updateScene; prevents Excalidraw
  from holding Y.Map's stored objects and mutating them during move/resize,
  which was causing move operations to never emit changes to peers
- Observer origin guard: only fire updateScene for 'remote'/'init' transactions,
  not local ones, to prevent handleChange → observer → handleChange loops
- fingerprint (id:versionNonce) guard in handleChange to skip no-op onChange
  calls (e.g. appState-only changes) and prevent re-emitting remote state
- Server-side lazy migration: getOrCreate detects old Y.Array('elements')
  snapshots and migrates them to Y.Map/Y.Array on first load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Replace split('-') with indexOf + substring in parseRevision() to avoid
array allocation. Replace parseInt + substring with manual charCode loop
for multi-digit heights in both parseRevision() and getHeightOfRevision().

- getHeightOfRevision multi-digit: 124-160% faster

commit.message:
perf: optimize now() with integer counter tracking (#8152)

* perf: optimize now() by replacing Math.round with integer counter tracking

Replace floating point arithmetic and Math.round(ret * 100) / 100 rounding
with separate integer tracking of milliseconds and sub-millisecond counter.
This eliminates the need for Math.round() entirely and reduces floating point
operations, achieving ~4.5% faster execution in benchmarks.

Before (1M calls): 56.04ms median
After  (1M calls): 53.64ms median

Co-authored-by: pubkey <8926560+pubkey@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(stream): race condition in pub/sub + migration for missing index

## pub/sub race condition (slow message delivery after reconnect)

spawn_inbox_wakeup() runs in a background task and takes ~50 ms to
complete TCP-connect + SUBSCRIBE. If a message arrived during that
window, its PUBLISH was missed, and the next delivery was at the
fallback poll — previously 30 s.

Fixes:
1. Synthetic wakeup sent immediately after SUBSCRIBE completes.
   This triggers an extra poll_messages() that catches any messages
   dispatched during the subscribe race window. Also fires on every
   pub/sub reconnect (desirable — catches messages missed during drop).

2. Fallback poll interval: 30 s → 5 s.
   Caps worst-case delivery delay (concurrent reconnects, Redis blip)
   without flooding the shared queue mutex.

## migration 033: missing index on user_blocks(blocked_user_id)

Migration 019 created the user_blocks composite PK
(blocker_user_id, blocked_user_id). Only the left column is indexed.
Queries like 'who has blocked me?' (is_blocked_by in shared/db.rs)
filter by blocked_user_id and caused full sequential scans.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(messaging): batch Redis ops into single lock + add timing spans

dispatch_envelope (Kafka-disabled path) previously acquired the queue
mutex THREE times sequentially: dedup-check, stream-write, sender-store.
Between each lock/unlock tokio could yield to poll_messages, causing
unnecessary contention and context-switch overhead on every send.

Changes:
- Kafka-disabled path: single mutex acquisition for all 3 Redis ops
  (dedup mark → XADD+PUBLISH → store_sender), released once at the end
- Kafka fallback path: stream-write + sender-store combined in one lock
- send_message RPC: log dispatch_ms after dispatch_envelope returns
- dispatch_envelope: log wait_ms (time to acquire lock) + redis_ms (batch)
- poll_messages: log lock_wait_ms + xread_ms when either exceeds 20 ms
- fix(sentinel): clippy dead_code, needless_question_mark, unnecessary_cast

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: construct-server
repository.description: post-modern server for exchange of data

commit.message:
perf(collab): revert and rollback to 60fps

- Client CURSOR_THROTTLE_MS: 33ms → 16ms (~60fps)
- Server CURSOR_THROTTLE_MS: 33ms → 16ms (~60fps)
- Added 16ms Yjs emit throttle with Y.mergeUpdates to batch rapid drag
  updates before sending (reduces socket emissions from ~120/s to ~60/s)
- Removed unused y-protocols package

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: add missing GET rule for /_template/:template_name permission path (#290)

* Initial plan

* fix: add missing GET method for /_template/:template_name permission path rule

The dev tools proxy reported 'unknown request path' for GET _template/legacy_logs
because the template permission section was missing a GET route. Added template.get
entry to allow GET requests to /_template/:template_name.

Co-authored-by: medcl <64487+medcl@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/infinilabs/console/sessions/7d821df3-2251-45ea-a8c0-08db564fbe75

* feat: add missing easysearch API permission rules for proxy routing

Add permission path rules for APIs documented in the easysearch REST API
reference that were missing from permission.json, causing 'unknown request
path' errors when accessed via the dev tools proxy.

New API categories added:
- async_search (submit, get, delete, stats)
- index_template (get, put, delete, exists, simulate)
- component_template (get, put, delete, exists)
- data_stream (get, create, delete, stats)
- ilm (get/put/delete policy, explain, add, change, remove, retry)
- resolve (resolve index)
- dangling_indices (list, import, delete)
- pit (create, delete, get all)
- search_pipeline (get, put, delete)
- transform (get, create, put, delete, explain, preview, start, stop)
- license (get, apply, delete)

Enhanced existing categories:
- cat: added parameterized routes (allocation/node_id, count/index, etc.)
- cluster: added _aliases POST, _cache/clear, _flush, _forcemerge, _refresh,
  _field_caps, _settings, _validate/query, _upgrade, _analyze, voting config,
  _ik/_reload, _refresh_search_analyzers, search_template, etc.
- indices: added clone, add_block, disk_usage, update_aliases, search_template,
  reload, and additional parameterized routes
- ingest: added per-pipeline ID routes for get/put/delete/simulate
- nodes: added parameterized stats/info/usage/hot_threads routes
- template: added GET all, HEAD exists, POST method
- rollup: added get, exists, start, stop, search routes
- scripts: added put with context, script_context, script_language
- snapshot: added list all, cleanup, clone, status with params
- reindex: added delete_by_query and update_by_query rethrottle
- sql: added explain, stats, settings
- tasks: added cancel all

Co-authored-by: medcl <64487+medcl@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/infinilabs/console/sessions/75cef488-e427-495a-b094-cb34809c0f5f

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: medcl <64487+medcl@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve absolute path for otel-hook in setup.sh (#36)

When otel-hook is on the shell PATH, setup.sh now writes the fully
resolved absolute path (e.g. /usr/local/bin/otel-hook) into hooks.json
instead of the bare command name. This ensures IDEs like Cursor that
don't inherit the shell PATH can still find the binary.

Changes:
- setup.sh: Use $(command -v otel-hook) to resolve absolute path
- setup.sh: Update LOG_HOME check to match absolute path pattern
- tests: Update assertion to expect absolute path


Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/o11y-dev/opentelemetry-hooks/sessions/df37adf1-56fd-4d11-88ce-cc2132eace04

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: semyont <6229585+semyont@users.noreply.github.com>
